summary.intent: Replace a manual MIME type lookup table in PR #464 with the `mime_guess` crate, then address all PR review comments (security and code quality issues).
summary.outcome: Successfully replaced the manual match with `mime_guess` (with fallbacks for edge cases), fixed a stored XSS vulnerability (HTML inline disposition), added missing blocked script extensions, warmed a blocking OnceLock at startup, and resolved all PR review comments. All changes committed and pushed.

repo: Workspace uses a `worktrees/moltis/feat/send-document-v2` path pattern for feature branches
`spawn_post_listener_warmups` in `crates/gateway/src/server.rs` is the established pattern for startup warmups
`moltis-web` crate tests cannot be run in isolation because they require a compiled Tailwind CSS asset
Nightly rustfmt (nightly-2025-11-30) requires trailing commas on `_ => {}` match arms
The repo uses a pre-commit hook system (`just format-check`) that runs fmt, biome, i18n, and zizmor checks in parallel

code.finding: mime_guess handles almost all extensions; only 'text', 'log' (→ text/plain) and 'ppm' (→ image/x-portable-pixmap) need manual fallbacks. mime_guess also recognizes .bmp, .svg, .xyz (chemical/x-xyz) which the old manual table did not.
BLOCKED_EXTENSIONS list was missing scripting language extensions: py, pyw, php, rb, pl, lua, tcl
text/html was incorrectly in the inline content-disposition allowlist, enabling stored XSS. HTML must force attachment/download.
container_cli() used std::process::Command inside OnceLock, blocking async workers on first call. Fixed by warming via spawn_blocking in spawn_post_listener_warmups.

workflow: mime_guess 2.0.5 was already a transitive dependency in the lockfile before being added as a direct dep
When replacing a manual extension→MIME table with a crate, existing tests asserting 'unsupported' for extensions the crate now recognizes need updating to use truly unknown extensions (e.g. .qqqq)
PR review comments from Greptile and human reviewers can be fetched and replied to via `gh api` to mark conversations as resolved

friction: mime_guess recognizes .bmp, .svg, and .xyz which broke existing tests asserting those returned None — required finding truly unknown extensions for negative test cases
moltis-web crate tests couldn't be run in isolation due to missing compiled CSS asset, making it impossible to verify the content-disposition test fix locally
Nightly rustfmt required `_ => {},` trailing comma that cargo fmt (stable) did not enforce, causing CI failure after the fact
mime_guess has no built-in reverse lookup returning a single preferred extension — the extension_for_mime function had to remain manual
open_items: extension_for_mime is still a manual match table with opinionated preferred extensions (e.g. 'jpg' not 'jpeg') — could be reconsidered but kept intentionally
content_disposition_attachment_for_html test was updated but could not be verified via test run due to CSS build dependency
mime_guess does not recognize 'text' or 'log' as extensions for text/plain — these edge cases are handled via manual fallback but worth noting if the extension set grows
